title: Live session auth chain with on_mount hooks and handle_params authz
prompt: Write two files: (1) lib/my_app_web/user_auth.ex (module MyAppWeb.UserAuth) with three on_mount hooks: :fetch_current_user (loads current_user from session token), :ensure_authenticated (redirects to ~p"/users/log_in" if not logged in, otherwise {:cont, socket}), :ensure_admin (halts with {:halt, push_navigate(socket, to: ~p"/")} if current_user.role != "admin"). (2) lib/my_app_web/live/admin/dashboard_live.ex (module MyAppWeb.Admin.DashboardLive). The Admin dashboard must handle_params/3 to additionally verify the requested ?org_id= belongs to the current_user.organizations — if not, push_navigate(socket, to: ~p"/") with put_flash(:error, "unauthorized"). Do NOT do auth checks in mount/3 — use on_mount hooks and handle_params authz. The router is NOT included in the output; only the UserAuth module and the LiveView.

must_not_contain:
def mount(_params, _session, socket) do
    if is_nil

def mount(_params, _session, socket) do
    case Accounts.authenticate

tier_rationale: Requires the on_mount hook return-value contract ({:cont, _} vs {:halt, _}) AND the architectural rule that auth belongs in hooks (not mount) AND per-request authz in handle_params (not mount). Sonnet typically puts all the auth logic directly in mount/3 or forgets the halt tuple.
research_citation: fly.io Phoenix LiveView auth guide; georgeguimaraes: 'on_mount hooks return {:cont, socket} or {:halt, push_navigate(...)}'
